title: phase inputs outputs verify catalog refs
purpose: C2.5 defines phase inputs outputs verify catalog refs for the agent-driven expert system. Product execution — pipelines, tasks, program, delivery.
scope: Owns `C2.5` only; siblings under `C2` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §5 — Branch C — Product execution plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#5-branch-c-product-execution-plane).
behavior_steps: When a company or program goal enters pursuit after H1, program-scoper loads `company.yaml` from the selected template-pack and dual-writes `state.company.pack_id`, default pipeline, and initial `active_role` into state.json.

Before the first product turn, S0 preflight validates that company.yaml references only sibling pack artifacts—[F1.2](F1.2-pack-roles---yaml.md) roles, [F1.3](F1.3-pack-pipelines---yaml.md) pipelines, and [F1.8](F1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md) verify suites—rejecting ad hoc consumer-repo extensions at H2.

During company autopilot ([A3.3](A3.3-company-autopilot-multi-goal-role-workstreams.md)), the conductor reloads department and role topology from the bound pack so orchestrate-program lanes and handoffs align with pack structure without duplicating org charts in journal prose.

Consumer goals pin pack version in pursuit state; the conductor imports pack metadata by reference rather than forking `company.yaml` inline, keeping template-packs the organizational ceiling per [F5.3](F5.3-no-repo-outside-template-packs-ceiling.md).

If schema drift, missing role references, or pack conformance failures surface during preflight or role switch, pursuit stops at H2 until pack metadata is reconciled and dual-written—never advance `next_action` on ambiguous org binding.

**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.

acceptance: [ ] Named script, skill, or test path exists or is listed in SEC-15 release row
narrative: C2.5 defines phase inputs outputs verify catalog refs for the agent-driven expert system. Product execution — pipelines, tasks, program, delivery. In practice, the system when a company or program goal enters pursuit after h1, program-scoper loads `company.yaml` from the selected template-pack and dual-writes `state.company.pack_id`, default pipeline, and initial `active_role` into state.json, then before the first product turn, s0 preflight validates that company.yaml references only sibling pack artifacts—[f1.2](f1.2-pack-roles---yaml.md) roles, [f1.3](f1.3-pack-pipelines---yaml.md) pipelines, and [f1.8](f1.8-pack-verify-goal-verify-suites.md) verify suites—rejecting ad hoc consumer-repo extensions at h2.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-c 2026-06-28 -->

Each phase declares inputs, outputs, verify commands, and catalog refs so S0 and reviewers can audit completeness. Phase contracts live in skills and playbooks; pack schema may extend with pack-specific verify.

Missing catalog refs on phase outputs trigger compose-first divergence logging ([B4.4](B4.4-divergence-log-when-not-composing.md)). Phase verify is not goal_verify—it proves phase artifacts exist before next_action advances.
